comment.body: Price moved out of negative gamma to positive gamma where hedging dynamics flipped. The positive gamma affect will have market makers hedging against price (in this case hedging against the decline in price). Now going back into negative gamma territory and back to positive could be the fight of the day. Negative gamma will see MM hedge with the price direction
comment.raw_body: Price moved out of negative gamma to positive gamma where hedging dynamics flipped. comment.body:
as you may have seen already, among retail traders there is a very bad behavior that i call "whos right" game.
they always want to be right on every moves, literally try to predict every steps of the white noise-ish intraday price action, that is
1) impossible, 2) unnecessary, 3) ridiculous

comment.body:
and as a result of this mentality, when a sudden up- or downstick happens, bcs some whale placed a huge fat order on the market, they try to catch it... after... 
they are angry why nobody said it, why they weren't able to predict it, and they start to chase... 
and then the candle goes away and grabs the liquidity.
it is usually the goal with those orders, to juice vol a bit
